articles.title: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT: “I’M GOING TO BE WORLD CHAMPION—ANYONE CAN GET IT!” 🔥
articles.description: After submitting Benoit Saint Denis at UFC 329, Paddy Pimblett made his championship ambitions crystal clear. When asked who he wants next, “The Baddy” said the opponent does not matter: “I want to be the first-ever world champion from Liverpool, and I’m going to do that.” Whether it takes one more fight or ten, Paddy believes becoming undisputed UFC champion is his destiny—and he says anyone can get it, including Justin Gaethje or Charles Oliveira. articles.title: 🚨 WHAT MOTIVATES PADDY PIMBLETT?!!! 🔥
articles.description: Paddy Pimblett revealed the real motivation behind his spectacular UFC 329 submission victory over Benoit Saint Denis. A reporter asked whether England’s World Cup win fired him up before the fight, but Paddy said his motivation comes from somewhere much closer to home: “I have little twin babies at home, and they give me more motivation than anything in this world.” Pimblett said everything he does is about providing for his children, putting food in their mouths, clothes on their backs, and continuing to win. Family first. articles.description: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT INVENTED THE FINISH ON THE FLY! “MY JIU-JITSU IS THE BEST IN THE WORLD!” 🔥 Paddy Pimblett revealed that his spectacular UFC 329 submission of Benoit Saint Denis was created in real time. articles.title: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT SPOTTED MIKE TYSON CAGESIDE—“THERE’S NO WAY I CAN LOSE!” 😳🔥
articles.description: Paddy Pimblett says his UFC 329 walkout was one of the most unforgettable moments of his career. With Conor McGregor headlining the event, the arena was packed with stars—and Paddy said his boxing coach pointed out Mike Tyson, Vince Vaughn, Mel Gibson, and Theo Von singing and dancing during his entrance. “I thought, ‘There’s no way I can lose.’” But once the cage door closed, Paddy said everything changed: “There’s another man in front of me who is going to try and kill me, so I’m going to kill him first.” Pimblett then defeated Benoit Saint Denis with a spectacular submission. Is Paddy Pimblett becoming one of the UFC’s biggest stars? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: 🚨 MAX HOLLOWAY REVEALS WHAT CONOR MCGREGOR SAID TO HIM DURING THE FIGHT 😳🔥
articles.description: As Max was landing punches on the ground, Conor reportedly told him: “Fight, fight.” Holloway said he backed away to let McGregor stand—but Conor fell back down and the fight was stopped. Max praised McGregor’s toughness, said he did not want him taking unnecessary damage in front of his children, and wished him a speedy recovery: "I don't want to see him take unnecessary damage trying to stop the fight, but this is how crazy Connor is. The first time he was on the ground, I was punching him. He said, "Fight, fight." I said, "Okay, that's why I backed up. Turn up then. Let's fight." Then he fell back down, and the fight was called." Why Do You Think Conor Said FIGHT to Max While He Was INJURED? 👇 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: 🚨 MAX HOLLOWAY NOTICED SOMETHING WAS DIFFERENT WITH CONOR BEFORE UFC 329 FIGHT 👀
articles.description: Max Holloway says Conor McGregor did not seem like his usual self before their UFC 329 fight. When asked whether the opening kick caused Conor’s knee injury, Holloway said he was not sure—but revealed that McGregor appeared unusually calm during his walk to the Octagon. "Even with him walking into the octagon, IT DIDN'T SEEM LIKE THE SAME CONOR.... I thought he would be a little more rowdy, a little more crazy. He looked really calm, super calm." Max expected McGregor to be louder, wilder, and more aggressive before the fight, but immediately noticed something felt different. articles.title: Max Holloway Has Every Advantage #ufc
articles.description: Who wins between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway? For Chael Sonnen, the answer is surprisingly simple. Chael believes there is no statistical or historical metric that favors McGregor in this matchup. To win, Conor wouldn't just have to overcome one obstacle—he'd have to overcome three challenges that no fighter has ever successfully conquered. First, there's the devastating leg injury. Several elite athletes have suffered similar breaks, and none returned looking like the same version of themselves. Second, no fighter has taken more than half a decade away from the sport and immediately returned to excel in a five-round fight. Third, nobody has come back after such a long layoff and defeated a current elite contender in his prime. And then there's one more problem: this fight takes place at a heavier weight class. Chael isn't questioning McGregor's greatness. In fact, he repeatedly acknowledges that Conor is capable of extraordinary things. But asking one man to overcome all of those hurdles simultaneously may simply be too much. That's why, when it comes time to make his prediction, Chael says he's not going to ignore history. Because picking McGregor means betting on something that has never happened before—three different times. articles.title: Conor and Max's INTENSE Faceoff #ufc
articles.description: The final faceoff between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway had everything fans have come to expect from McGregor—and one thing they didn't. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen reacts to an intense staredown that quickly became physical. McGregor has built a reputation for doing something memorable during faceoffs, whether it's getting in an opponent's space, flinching, or trying to establish dominance before the fight even begins. This time was no different. But Chael noticed something else. Throughout the evening, McGregor carried himself differently. The usual over-the-top entertainer wasn't fully on display. Instead, Chael saw a calmer, more measured, and more sincere version of Conor. Even after the heated exchange with Holloway, there was a sense that this wasn't simply promotion—it felt genuine. Chael has always appreciated McGregor's ability to create unforgettable moments, but this appearance stood out because it seemed to come from a different place. The showmanship was still there, but the authenticity came first. As fight night approaches, Chael believes that's an important detail. A focused and sincere McGregor may be every bit as dangerous as the loud, brash superstar fans remember. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, Max Holloway news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #FaceOff #CombatSports #UFCReturn

articles.title: Stop Trying to Make Josh Hokit vs. Gable Steveson
articles.description: Josh Hokit is undefeated and already ranked among the UFC's top heavyweights. Gable Steveson is one of the most decorated wrestling prospects to ever enter MMA. They're also teammates. So why is everyone already trying to make them fight? Chael Sonnen explains why the rush to create a rivalry between Hokit and Gable annoys him, especially at a time when elite wrestling has never been more underrepresented in the UFC. Drawing comparisons to Jon Jones and Rashad Evans, as well as Chuck Liddell and Tito Ortiz, Chael warns how quickly questions about rankings and who gets the better of whom in the practice room can create real problems inside a team. Gable hasn't even made his UFC debut yet. Give him a chance to start his journey before trying to tear two teammates apart. articles.title: Conor McGregor vs. Justin Gaethje? #ufc
articles.description: According to Chael Sonnen, there may be no more intriguing matchup in the UFC than Conor McGregor versus Justin Gaethje. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Gaethje's style could actually favor McGregor despite Justin being one of the most dangerous fighters in the sport. Yes, Gaethje is an All-American wrestler and a black belt in jiu-jitsu, but he rarely uses either offensively. Instead, he prefers to stand and trade punches—and that's exactly the kind of fight that could give Conor a chance. Chael isn't saying McGregor is the better fighter. In fact, he calls Gaethje the nastiest man in the world and recognizes him as one of the UFC's elite champions. But styles make fights, and Chael believes this particular matchup is fascinating. If McGregor were to defeat Max Holloway, Chael predicts the UFC would immediately book a showdown with Gaethje. The fight would make perfect sense for everyone involved. Gaethje deserves another massive opportunity, and McGregor would gladly accept it. The path to victory for Conor would remain the same: he isn't likely to outlast Gaethje over five rounds or submit him on the ground. If McGregor wins, Chael believes it would have to come by knockout. And that's exactly why this fight is so compelling. articles.title: The Only Path to Victory for Conor #ufc
articles.description: According to Chael Sonnen, there is only one way that Conor McGregor defeats Max Holloway—and it has to happen early. In this breakdown, Chael explains why he doesn't believe McGregor can win a long, drawn-out fight with Holloway. He argues there's no realistic scenario where Conor wins a five-round decision and no path that leads to a submission victory. If McGregor is going to get his hand raised, Chael believes it has to come by knockout. More specifically, it has to come in the opening round. Chael points to the unique challenges facing McGregor: a long layoff, competing at a higher weight class, and trying to match the pace of one of the sport's best-conditioned fighters. In his view, championship rounds heavily favor Holloway. But there's one great equalizer—the left hand. Can McGregor still land it? Does he still have the speed, timing, and setups that made him one of the most dangerous strikers in UFC history? Chael believes those are the real questions. Because if Conor still has that weapon, he still has a chance. And Chael suspects McGregor sees it the same way. If Conor took this fight, he likely believes he can get Max out of there before the fight becomes a battle of endurance. articles.title: Nobody Sells Fights Like Conor #ufc
articles.description: It doesn't matter who Conor McGregor fights. According to Chael Sonnen, he's the only athlete in combat sports who can truly make that claim. In this breakdown, Chael explains why McGregor's star power goes beyond opponents, rankings, and even weight classes. Drawing comparisons to legends like Mike Tyson, Oscar De La Hoya, and Floyd Mayweather Jr., Chael argues that Conor belongs in the rare category of athletes who can sell out arenas simply by showing up. But this comeback isn't just about popularity—it's about attempting something that has never been done. Chael lays out the three enormous obstacles facing McGregor. He must return from a devastating leg injury that fighters like Chris Weidman and Anderson Silva never fully overcame. He has to compete outside of his natural weight class against an elite opponent. And he must return after nearly six years away from active competition and immediately defeat a current top contender. No one has successfully done all of those things. That's exactly why Chael believes this fight is so compelling. McGregor understands the challenge in front of him and still chose one of the most dangerous opponents available. The stakes, the risk, and the possibility of history make this one of the biggest fights the sport has ever seen. articles.title: Have We Forgotten How Great Conor McGregor Actually Is? #ufc
articles.description: What will the legacy of Conor McGregor ultimately be? In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen asks a fascinating question: have fans forgotten just how incredible McGregor's accomplishments really were? Becoming a simultaneous UFC champion at both featherweight and lightweight is one of the rarest achievements in combat sports history, especially considering how deep and competitive those divisions have always been. Chael revisits some of Conor's legendary moments, including his interim title victory over Chad Mendes and his historic 13-second knockout of José Aldo, widely regarded as one of the greatest featherweights of all time. He also reflects on McGregor's lightweight championship win over Eddie Alvarez, a performance many consider one of the finest in UFC history. Then there was the boxing match against Floyd Mayweather Jr., where McGregor stepped into an entirely different sport and went ten rounds with one of boxing's all-time greats. Beyond the championships and the records, Chael believes another part of McGregor's story deserves recognition: his refusal to let go of his identity as a fighter. Even through setbacks and difficult moments, Conor's passion for competition has never disappeared. For Chael, when the story of Conor McGregor is finally written, the conversation may not just be about fame or money. It may be about one of the most talented fighters the sport has ever seen. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen #JoseAldo #EddieAlvarez #ChadMendes #MMA #CombatSports

articles.description: In a SPECIAL EDITION of the Daniel Cormier Show, DC sits down with PADDY "The Baddy" PIMBLETT ahead of his massive fight against Benoit Saint-Denis at the UFC's International Fight Week on a card that features Conor McGregor and Max Holloway! Pimblett breaks down why he'll beat Benoit convincingly on Saturday night. Hear Paddy give his hilarious reaction to Justin Gaethje defeating Ilia Topuria at UFC Freedom 250 and go off on Ilia "QUITTING" in the corner. Plus, hear Pimblett's incredible story of how he started pro MMA at such a young age in Liverpool and why his relentless confidence has made him the superstar he is today. And don't miss Paddy talk about his Baddy Foundation, his uncle playing for Aston Villa, and DC's $1,000 LOSING BET with Paddy Pimblett on the World Cup! Timeline: 0:00 - Upbringing in Liverpool 8:00 - Starting to take up fighting 11:00 - Almost quitting fighting early on 13:00 - His uncle's soccer career 15:30 - Turning down the UFC 19:00 - Winning featherweight belt in Liverpool 21:00 - UFC debut 25:30 - Losing to Justin Gaethje & Ilia Topuria "QUIT" 33:00 - Fighting Benoit Saint Denis Saturday 36:00 - DC's terrible USA World Cup bet 41:30 - Losing his friend before his fight & Baddy Foundation All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms!
